tool_args.label: MTA 2026 Final Proposed Budget, Vol 1
tool_args.raw_text: MTA 2026 Final Proposed Budget, November Financial Plan 2026 – 2029, Volume 1, November 2025. Metropolitan Transportation Authority.

payload.title: MTA 2026 Final Proposed Budget
payload.description: November Financial Plan 2026-2029 Volume 1, consisting of financial schedules supporting the MTA Consolidated Financial Plan, including Executive Summary, baseline forecast, below-the-line adjustments, fare and toll increases, MTA Initiatives, and Budget Process.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access
tool_args.raw_text: Full completion of the Penn Station Access project, which extends Metro-North's New Haven Line to reach Penn Station with four new accessible stations in the Bronx, has experienced delays due to coordination issues with Amtrak for work on its right-of-way. The full completion of the project, originally scheduled for 2027, is now expected to be delayed until 2030. MTA is planning to provide about 30 percent of the original scheduled service to three of the four new Bronx stations.

payload.description: Full completion of the project, which extends Metro-North's New Haven Line to reach Penn Station with four new accessible stations in the Bronx, originally scheduled for 2027, now expected to be delayed until 2030 due to coordination issues with Amtrak for work on its right-of-way. MTA planning to provide about 30 percent of original scheduled service to three of the four new Bronx stations as interim plan.

tool_args.label: November Plan balanced through 2026
tool_args.raw_text: The November Plan remains balanced through 2026 with deficits of $160 million in 2027, $243 million in 2028, and $306 million in 2029.

payload.claim_text: The November Plan remains balanced through 2026 with deficits of $160 million in 2027, $243 million in 2028, and $306 million in 2029.
payload.subject: MTA November Financial Plan 2026-2029

tool_args.label: $3.2B recurring savings captured 2009-2022
tool_args.raw_text: From 2009 through 2022, the MTA identified and captured about $3.2 billion in annually recurring savings.

payload.claim_text: From 2009 through 2022, the MTA identified and captured about $3.2 billion in annually recurring savings.
payload.subject: MTA savings initiatives

tool_args.label: Additional $500M recurring savings by end of 2025
tool_args.raw_text: In 2023, the MTA committed to achieving an additional $500 million in annually recurring cost savings through operating efficiencies, which was attained by the end of 2025.

payload.claim_text: In 2023, the MTA committed to achieving an additional $500 million in annually recurring cost savings through operating efficiencies, which was attained by the end of 2025.
payload.subject: MTA operating efficiencies

tool_args.label: New operating efficiency savings targets
tool_args.raw_text: Savings targets from new operating efficiencies are being introduced in this Plan. Achievable savings of $75 million in 2026, growing to $150 million in 2027 and $200 million in 2028, are expected to reach a full value of $250 million in annual recurring operating savings by 2029.

payload.claim_text: Savings targets from new operating efficiencies: $75 million in 2026, growing to $150 million in 2027 and $200 million in 2028, reaching $250 million in annual recurring operating savings by 2029.
payload.subject: MTA new operating efficiencies

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access delayed to 2030
tool_args.raw_text: Full completion of the Penn Station Access project, which extends Metro-North's New Haven Line to reach Penn Station with four new accessible stations in the Bronx, has experienced delays due to coordination issues with Amtrak for work on its right-of-way. The full completion of the project, originally scheduled for 2027, is now expected to be delayed until 2030.

payload.claim_text: Full completion of the Penn Station Access project, originally scheduled for 2027, is now expected to be delayed until 2030 due to coordination issues with Amtrak for work on its right-of-way.
payload.subject: Penn Station Access project

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access interim 30% service
tool_args.raw_text: MTA is planning to provide about 30 percent of the original scheduled service to three of the four new Bronx stations. This interim plan, which is predicated on cooperation by Amtrak, will result in shifts in operating budget spending, lowering net costs by $58 million over the Plan period.

payload.claim_text: MTA is planning to provide about 30 percent of the original scheduled service to three of the four new Bronx stations as interim service predicated on cooperation by Amtrak.
payload.subject: Penn Station Access interim service plan

tool_args.label: FEMA COVID reimbursement delay
tool_args.raw_text: In this Plan, the reimbursement is expected over the 2026 to 2028 period, with receipt of $200 million each year.

payload.claim_text: FEMA COVID reimbursement expected over 2026 to 2028 period, with receipt of $200 million each year, delayed from earlier expectations.
payload.subject: FEMA COVID reimbursement

tool_args.label: Proposed Fare and Toll Increase March 2027
tool_args.raw_text: March 2027 (4% Fare and Toll Yield) – An increase in fares and tolls, yielding a 4 percent overall increase in farebox and toll revenues, is assumed for implementation in March 2027

payload.description: Proposed fare and toll rate increase yielding 4% overall increase in farebox and toll revenues, projected to generate $287 million in 2027.

tool_args.label: Proposed Fare and Toll Increase March 2029
tool_args.raw_text: March 2029 (4% Fare and Toll Yield) – An increase in fares and tolls, yielding a 4% overall increase in farebox and toll revenues, is assumed for implementation in March 2029

payload.description: Proposed fare and toll rate increase yielding 4% overall increase, projected to generate $302 million in 2029.

tool_args.label: Risk: Dedicated tax receipts economic sensitivity
tool_args.raw_text: Almost 45 percent of operating revenues that are necessary to cover operating budget expenses are derived from dedicated taxes, and an economic slowdown or recession could adversely impact MTA's ability to cover costs.

payload.claim_text: Almost 45 percent of operating revenues are derived from dedicated taxes, and an economic slowdown or recession could adversely impact MTA's ability to cover costs.
payload.subject: Risks to MTA's Financial Future

tool_args.label: Risk: Casino license revenue timing
tool_args.raw_text: Casino revenue. The awarding of casino licenses is expected by the end of 2025. Should the commencement of operations be delayed, the Plan, which assumes license and gaming tax revenues of $500 million in both 2026 and 2027, $600 million in 2028, and $200 million in 2029, will be out of balance.

payload.claim_text: The awarding of casino licenses is expected by the end of 2025. Should commencement of operations be delayed, the Plan will be out of balance. Plan assumes license and gaming tax revenues of $500 million in 2026 and 2027, $600 million in 2028, and $200 million in 2029.
payload.subject: Risks to MTA's Financial Future

tool_args.label: Risk: Fare and toll increase approval
tool_args.raw_text: The Plan includes fare and toll rate increases proposed for March 2027 and March 2029. These increases are expected to generate $1.22 billion over the Plan period.

payload.claim_text: The Plan includes fare and toll rate increases proposed for March 2027 and March 2029, expected to generate $1.22 billion over the Plan period.
payload.subject: Risks to MTA's Financial Future
